metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1358
metadata.comment:
Rationale: ARL6IP5 (GTRAP3-18) inhibits glutamate uptake through EAAC1. Figure1C that EAAC1 and GTRAP3-18 can be reversibly be IPed from rat brain extract. Figure 3A shows that coexpression of GTRAP3 with EAAC1 in HEK293 cells reduced tritiated-glutamate uptake. Suppressing the GTRAP3 protein expression by antisense oligo injection increased tritiated glutamate uptake in HEK cells (Fig3D) and rat cortex (Fig3E and F). Figure 4 shows that using retinoic acid GTRAP3-18 expression is induced in HEK cells which results in a dose dependent decrease reduction in glutamate uptake (Fig3A), which is reproduced in vivo rat brain (Fig4E) and could be reversed by antisense oligo injection. The bottom pannel of Fig4E shows that the effect is insensitive to DHK, which blocks EAAC2 but EAAC1 is relative insensitive (supp reference).

24/10/2017 Pim
- With Fig.4 the authors include experiments that show the action of GTRAP3-18 in the brain via shRNA intervention.. Experimental description: Figure legends: 1C "Western blot (WB) analyses of immunoprecipitations (IP) from rat brain extracts" 3F "Kinetic analysis of cortical tissue from rats treated... " 4f " Kinetic analysis of cortical uptake from rats treated with retinoic acid..."

24/10/2017 Pim
- Over-expressed protein was isolated and cloned from a rat cDNA library.
